project.title: LC-ESI-MS/MS on a purified recombinant protein sample to confirm its amino acid sequence and a certain cleavage site
project.projectDescription: The study entails the design, production and in vitro testing of a novel recombinant anti-B7-H3 affibody coupled with a small cytotoxic protein, with considerable potential in Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML) immunotherapy. The protein construct was expressed in E. coli. After affinity purification and validation of its sequence through LC-MS/MS, the obtained protein’s efficacy was tested on AML representative B7-H3-positive THP-1 cells and B7-H3-negative RAJI cells. The IC50 of the cytotoxic protein was determined through MTS assay and its apoptotic and necrotic activities on the two cell lines were evaluated through flow cytometry and Western blot. Taken together, the results show that the cytotoxic anti-B7-H3 affibody we produced possesses highly necrotic and apoptotic targeted effects that are specific to only B7-H3-expressing THP-1 cells and not the RAJI control line. Moreover, these observed effects are significantly more potent than the effects of the cytotoxin alone, as they are described in previous studies. In conclusion, we designed, produced and evaluated a novel anti-B7-H3 cytotoxic affibody that from these preliminary in vitro data, shows high potential for translation to the immunotherapy of AML.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: The purified protein sample used was in a 50 mM Tris, 300 mM NaCl, 1 mM dithiothreitol (DTT) buffer. For processing, an in-solution trypsin digestion was employed. First, the protein sample was denatured in denaturation buffer (final concentration of 8 M urea in 100 mM Tris-HCl). Then, a sufficient volume of protein reduction buffer (100 mM DTT in 50 mM ammonium bicarbonate – ABC) was added to the sample, until the final DTT concentration reached 10 mM and the sample was incubated for one hour at room temperature with moderate shaking. After reduction, the sample was alkylated in a final concentration of 55 mM iodoacetamide in 50 mM ABC buffer, by incubating it for one hour at room temperature with moderate shaking. This was followed by inactivation of the alkylating agent by adding protein reduction buffer in two times the volume of previously added alkylating buffer for one hour at room temperature with moderate shaking. Then, urea in the sample was diluted to < 1.0 M, by adding 50 mM ABC, which was followed by adjusting the pH of the sample to approximately 7.7 with 50 mM ABC, for optimal trypsin digestion. Trypsin digestion was performed by adding trypsin buffer (100 ng/µL sequencing grade trypsin from Promega, cat. no. V5111 in 50 mM acetic acid) until the w:w ratio of trypsin:protein in the sample was 1:50 and then incubating the sample for 16 hours at 37 °C. Subsequently, the sample was desalted as previously described and concentrated to dryness until injection. For nanoLC-MS/MS analysis the sample was reconstituted in solvent A (0.06% formic acid – FA and 2% acetonitrile – ACN) and subsequently analyzed using an Easy-nanoLC 1200 coupled online to an LTQ-Orbitrap Velos Pro instrument. For data acquisition a data dependent strategy was used involving CID/HCD peptide fragmentation in separate runs.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The generated raw data were analyzed using SequestHT algorithm integrated into Proteome Disocverer v2.5 (ThermoFisher Scientific) against E. Coli and its subtaxonomies proteome to which the recombinant sequence was appended, using the following parameters: 10 ppm for precursor mass tolerance, 0.5 Da for fragment mass tolerance for MS/MS spectra acquired in the linear trap and 0.02 Da for MS/MS spectra acquired in the Orbitrap, enzyme used Trypsin (considering semitryptic specificity) with a maximum of two missed cleavages, Met oxidation as a variable modification and Cys Carbamidomethylation as a fixed modification. For spectra validation the Target/Decoy strategy was used with the default parameters considering only PSMs at 1% FDR.

references.referenceLine: Vasilescu AM, Vasilescu AG, Sima LE, Munteanu CVA, Baran N, Szedlacsek ȘE. A novel cytotoxic anti-B7-H3 affibody with therapeutic potential in acute myeloid leukemia. Front Pharmacol. 2025 16:1684226

Characteristics[MaterialType]: purified protein sample in Dialysis Buffer
Characteristics[ReductionReagent]: 10 mM DTT in 50 mM ammonium bicarbonate (ABC)
Comment[Instrument]: an Easy-nLC 1200 coupled to an LTQ Orbitrap™ Velos Pro (Thermo Fisher Scientific) mass spectrometer was used
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: The data acquisition involved a data-dependent procedure under which the top five most abundant ions from the survey scan acquired in the orbital trap were selected for fragmentation using either CID or HCD, alternatively, in different runs, as previously described.
Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance]: 10 ppm maximum allowed mass deviation for precursors
Comment[FragmentMassTolerance]: 0.5 Da for the fragments mass tolerance acquired in the linear trap
0.02 Da for MS/MS ions acquired in the Orbitrap
Comment[Separation]: separated on an RP C18 Acclaim™ PepMap™ 100 (0.075 um x 150 mm) analytical column (Thermo Fisher Scientific) using a 2%–30% solvent B (0.06% FA and 80% ACN) for ∼90 min
